paper_title: New data on taxonomy and systematics of the genus Diamesa Meigen (Diptera: Chironomidae: Diamesinae) from Tien Shan and Pamir Mountains, with description of two new species

methods.description: Preservation of biological material for subsequent DNA analysis and morphological study, including slide-mounting.

steps.description: The material was preserved in 96% ethanol for DNA analysis
steps.searchable_summary: preserve biological samples in 96% ethanol for DNA analysis

steps.description: and in 70% ethanol for further study of morphology and slide-mounting, following the methods by Makarchenko (1985).
steps.searchable_summary: preserve biological samples in 70% ethanol for morphology study and slide-mounting

steps.description: slide-mounting, following the methods by Makarchenko (1985).
steps.searchable_summary: perform slide-mounting of biological samples following a specific protocol

methods.description: Extraction of total genomic DNA from individual chironomid specimens using a commercial kit.

steps.description: Total genomic DNA was extracted from thorax of the 27 individual specimens of chironomids
steps.searchable_summary: extract total genomic DNA from insect thorax tissue

steps.description: using the Invitrogen PureLink Genomic DNA Mini Kit
steps.searchable_summary: use Invitrogen PureLink Genomic DNA Mini Kit for extraction
inputs: Invitrogen PureLink Genomic DNA Mini Kit

steps.description: in compliance with the manufacturer's protocols.
steps.searchable_summary: follow manufacturer's protocol for DNA extraction kit
inputs: Invitrogen PureLink Genomic DNA Mini Kit manufacturer's protocols

methods.description: Amplification of specific mitochondrial and nuclear DNA markers using PCR and subsequent bidirectional Sanger sequencing.

steps.description: Primers for PCR amplification on three mitochondrial and two nuclear markers given in Tab.S1.
steps.searchable_summary: perform PCR amplification using specified primers for mitochondrial and nuclear markers

steps.description: The PCR conditions and thermal regime given in Makarchenko et al. (2017),
steps.searchable_summary: apply PCR conditions and thermal regime from a reference publication
inputs: PCR conditions and thermal regime (Makarchenko et al., 2017)

steps.description: but the elongation time varied in accordance with the length of the amplicons.
steps.searchable_summary: adjust PCR elongation time based on amplicon length

steps.description: The PCR products were bidirectionally sequenced using the BigDye Terminator v3.1 cycle kit
steps.searchable_summary: perform bidirectional DNA sequencing using BigDye Terminator kit
outputs: Raw sequence data (forward and reverse reads)

steps.description: and run on an ABI 3130xl (Applied Biosystems™).
steps.searchable_summary: run DNA sequencing reactions on an ABI 3130xl genetic analyzer

methods.description: Processing of raw DNA sequence data including manual assembly, editing, and alignment.

steps.description: Sequences were manually assembled and edited using Finch TV and MEGA 7 (Kumar et al., 2016).
steps.searchable_summary: manually assemble DNA sequences from raw reads
inputs: Raw sequence data (forward and reverse reads)

steps.description: Sequences were manually assembled and edited using Finch TV and MEGA 7 (Kumar et al., 2016).
steps.searchable_summary: manually edit assembled DNA sequences

steps.description: The obtained sequences were checked aligned at the nucleotide level using MUSCLE (Edgar, 2004).
steps.searchable_summary: align nucleotide sequences using MUSCLE

methods.description: Estimation of genetic distances between sequences using the Kimura 2 parameter model.

steps.description: To estimation the Kimura 2 parameter (K2P) distances we use MEGA 7 (Kumar et al., 2016).
steps.searchable_summary: calculate Kimura 2 parameter (K2P) genetic distances

methods.description: Selection of the best-fit partitioning scheme and substitution models for phylogenetic analysis using PartitionFinder.

steps.description: PartitionFinder 2.1.1 (Lanfear et al., 2012) was used to select the best-fit partitioning scheme and models
steps.searchable_summary: select phylogenetic partitioning scheme and substitution models using PartitionFinder

steps.description: separately for each codon position of protein coding genes as well as ribosomal loci
steps.searchable_summary: select models separately for different partitions (codon positions, ribosomal loci)
inputs: Alignment partitions (e.g., codon positions, ribosomal loci)

steps.description: using the greedy algorithm
steps.searchable_summary: use the greedy algorithm for model selection in PartitionFinder

steps.description: with linked branch lengths
steps.searchable_summary: link branch lengths across partitions during model selection

steps.description: for the corrected Akaike Information Criterion as the optimality criterion for model selection.
steps.searchable_summary: use corrected Akaike Information Criterion (AICc) as the model selection criterion

methods.description: Performing Bayesian phylogenetic analysis using MrBayes with Metropolis-coupled Markov chain Monte Carlo.

steps.description: Bayesian phylogenetic analyses were conducted with MrBayes v. 3.2.6 (Ronquist and Huelsenbeck, 2003).
steps.searchable_summary: conduct Bayesian phylogenetic analysis using MrBayes

steps.description: Bayesian Inference was performed with two independent runs of Metropolis-coupled Markov chain Monte Carlo analyses,
steps.searchable_summary: perform multiple independent MCMC runs for Bayesian phylogeny

steps.description: with each run comprising one cold chain and three heated chains at a default temperature of 0.1.
steps.searchable_summary: configure MCMC chains (cold, heated, temperature) for Bayesian analysis

steps.description: The chains were run for 10 million generations
steps.searchable_summary: run MCMC chains for a specified number of generations

steps.description: and sampled every 100 generations.
steps.searchable_summary: sample MCMC tree samples every N generations

methods.description: Processing the results of Bayesian phylogenetic inference, including applying burn-in and inspecting trace files for convergence.

steps.description: A burn-in of 100 000 generations (or 1% of the sampled trees) was used.
steps.searchable_summary: apply burn-in to MCMC phylogenetic samples

steps.description: Moreover, trace files were visually inspected in Tracer 1.7 (Rambaut et al., 2018).
steps.searchable_summary: visually inspect MCMC trace files for convergence using Tracer
